title_en: Britain’s bookies have weathered a tricky World Cup
title_zh: 英国博彩公司挺过了一届棘手的世界杯
rubric_en: But bigger problems are stacking up
rubric_zh: 但更大的问题正在越积越多

paragraphs.en: “We were lucky today,” said England’s manager, Thomas Tuchel, after his team’s nerve-shredding 2-1 win over Norway on July 11th. Britain’s bookmakers enjoyed their own good fortune: England reached the semi-finals of the World Cup, but for betting purposes the match was a draw. By convention, match wagers are settled on the 90-minute scoreline (1-1). England’s winning goal came in extra time, saving the bookies millions of pounds.
paragraphs.zh: 7月11日，英格兰队以2比1惊险战胜挪威队后，主教练托马斯·图赫尔说：“我们今天很幸运。”英国博彩公司也有自己的好运：英格兰进入了世界杯半决赛，但从投注结算的角度看，这场比赛算作平局。按照惯例，比赛投注按90分钟比分结算（1比1）。英格兰的制胜球是在加时赛打进的，为博彩公司省下了数百万英镑。

paragraphs.en: Across this expanded 48-team World Cup, favourites have won 63% of matches in normal time, compared with 53% on average in the previous three. Underdogs prevailed in only 10%, against an average of 23% previously. Such results would once have perturbed British bookies, which do well when favourites don’t.
paragraphs.zh: 在这届扩军至48支球队的世界杯中，热门球队在常规时间内赢下了63%的比赛，而此前三届的平均比例为53%。冷门球队获胜的比例只有10%，此前平均为23%。这样的结果放在过去会让英国博彩公司不安，因为热门球队不赢时，它们才更赚钱。

paragraphs.en: Not this time. Bookies have steered many customers towards “bet builders” (or “same-game parlays” in America). At Kambi, a sportsbook supplier, these made up more than 30% of bets at recent football tournaments. Punters might bet on, say, England to win, Jude Bellingham to score, and the match to have over nine corners and more than two bookings, all in one wager. In the Norway game, only one of those outcomes occurred in regular time. Such bets help the bookies’ profits. Trading data shared with The Economist by four of them suggest that, despite match results generally favouring customers, all remain broadly on course to achieve their usual margins.
paragraphs.zh: 但这一次不是这样。博彩公司已把许多客户引向“串关组合投注”（在美国称为“同场串关”）。体育博彩供应商Kambi的数据显示，在最近几届足球锦标赛中，这类投注占比超过30%。赌客可能会把英格兰获胜、裘德·贝林厄姆进球、全场角球超过9个、黄牌等警告超过2次等选项放在同一注里。挪威那场比赛中，这些结果在常规时间内只发生了一个。这类投注有助于提高博彩公司利润。四家博彩公司向《经济学人》分享的交易数据显示，尽管比赛结果总体上有利于客户，但它们大体上仍有望实现通常的利润率。

paragraphs.en: Yet a decent World Cup will not dispel the industry’s broader worries. Tax and licensing reforms in the early 2000s were intended to allow gambling to flourish in the internet age. They succeeded, until the political wind changed. Last year’s budget raised the tax on online casino-style profits from 21% to 40%. From April 2027 most online sports betting will also face a tax increase, from 15% to 25%.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，一届还不错的世界杯并不能消除该行业更广泛的忧虑。21世纪初的税收和牌照改革，原本是为了让博彩业在互联网时代蓬勃发展。它们确实奏效了，直到政治风向发生变化。去年的预算把在线赌场类利润的税率从21%提高到40%。从2027年4月起，大多数在线体育博彩也将面临增税，税率从15%升至25%。

paragraphs.en: And the Gambling Commission, the industry regulator, has pressed ahead with “financial risk assessments”, intended to identify customers at risk of gambling harm. Bookies argue that the checks are intrusive and are driving customers to unlicensed operators. Anthony Kaminskas, founder of AK Bets, says 78% of his customers refuse to provide financial records when asked.
paragraphs.zh: 同时，行业监管机构赌博委员会推进了“财务风险评估”，旨在识别有赌博伤害风险的客户。博彩公司认为，这些审查侵犯隐私，并正在把客户推向无牌经营者。AK Bets创始人安东尼·卡明斯卡斯说，他的客户中有78%在被要求提供财务记录时拒绝配合。

paragraphs.en: Gambling’s new growth story is America. Flutter, the world’s largest online-betting company, and owner of British brands including Betfair and Sky Bet, will delist from the London Stock Exchange next month, making New York its sole stock-market home. Britain’s bookies have cracked the problem of predictable football results. Their bigger challenge lies in Westminster. ■
paragraphs.zh: 博彩业新的增长故事在美国。全球最大的在线博彩公司Flutter拥有Betfair和Sky Bet等英国品牌，它将于下个月从伦敦证券交易所退市，使纽约成为其唯一的股票市场所在地。英国博彩公司已经破解了足球赛果过于可预测这一难题。它们更大的挑战在威斯敏斯特。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in Britain, sign up to Blighty, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需更多关于英国重大新闻的专家分析，请订阅Blighty，这是我们的每周订阅者专享通讯。
